pi_items.metadata_value: p) Light Drill 1 - Power Driven, q) Light Drill 2 - Sailing Vessel, r) Light Drill 3 - Vessel Restricted Ability, s) Light Drill 4 - Fishing Vessel, t) Light Drill 5 - Vessel Aground
pi_items.verified_value: Scenario names match COLREGS rule structure and vessel type keywords

["23", "25", "26", "27", "30"] - Rule 23: Power-driven vessels, Rule 25: Sailing vessels, Rule 26: Fishing vessels, Rule 27: Vessels not under command/restricted ability, Rule 30: Anchored/aground vessels
pi_items.source: COLREGS official documentation and maritime regulations (ecolregs.com, USCG, Cornell Law, cultofsea.com)

pi_items.verified_value: ["power", "sailing", "restricted", "fishing", "aground"] - Each keyword corresponds to a COLREGS rule

pi_items.metadata_value: StartTime, VisibilityRange, Weather parameters used in environment.ini
pi_items.verified_value: StartTime (24-hour decimal format, e.g., 22.0 for 10:00 PM), VisibilityRange (nautical miles), Weather (0-12 scale) are documented Bridge Command environment.ini parameters
InitialSpeed in ownship.ini specifies vessel speed in knots, example: InitialSpeed=15 for 15 knots
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation and examples from ecolregs.com and bridgecommand.co.uk

pi_items.metadata_value: Number parameter for vessel count, Type(1) for vessel type
pi_items.verified_value: Number field specifies count of NPC vessels, Type(1), Type(2), etc. specify individual vessel models (e.g., Type(1)="HMAS_Waller", Type(1)="Timbercarrier")
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation and scenario examples

pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is a real, open-source maritime simulator (GPL-2.0, hosted at github.com/bridgecommand/bc, documented at bridgecommand.co.uk)
pi_items.source: GitHub repository https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc and official website https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk

pi_items.verified_value: COLREGS (Convention on International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea, 1972) is the real international maritime standard published by IMO
pi_items.source: IMO official website (imo.org), USCG Navigation Center (navcen.uscg.gov), US Code Title 33 CFR 83

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic training task (not based on real data) that uses the real Bridge Command maritime simulator and COLREGS (International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea). The task creates 5 navigation light drills corresponding to real COLREGS Rules: Rule 23 (Power-driven vessels), Rule 25 (Sailing vessels), Rule 26 (Fishing vessels), Rule 27 (Vessels restricted in ability to maneuver), and Rule 30 (Vessels aground). Bridge Command scenarios are stored in /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/ and configured via INI files (environment.ini, ownship.ini, othership.ini) with parameters like StartTime (24-hour decimal), VisibilityRange (nautical miles), InitialSpeed (knots), and vessel type specifications. All metadata claims about software structure, file formats, and COLREGS rule assignments have been verified against authoritative sources.
